treatment: Apply copper-based fungicide. Remove infected leaves. Avoid overhead watering.
prevention: Crop rotation, resistant varieties, proper spacing.

symptoms: dark water-soaked lesions, white mold under leaves
treatment: Apply metalaxyl or mancozeb. Destroy infected plants immediately.
prevention: Use certified seeds, avoid excess irrigation.

symptoms: water-soaked lesions, yellow halo, brown borders
treatment: Use copper oxychloride spray. Drain flooded fields. Apply potassium fertilizer.
prevention: Certified seeds, balanced fertilization, proper drainage.

symptoms: diamond-shaped gray lesions on leaves and neck
prevention: Resistant varieties, balanced nitrogen, avoid dense planting.

symptoms: orange-yellow pustules in stripes on leaves
treatment: Spray propiconazole or tebuconazole fungicide immediately.
prevention: Resistant varieties, early sowing, seed treatment.

symptoms: curling leaves, stunted growth, mosaic pattern
treatment: No direct cure. Remove infected plants. Control whitefly vector with neem oil.

symptoms: dark brown lesions on leaves, white mold under leaves
treatment: Spray mancozeb or metalaxyl. Destroy infected material.
prevention: Certified seed tubers, avoid excess irrigation.

symptoms: grayish-green to tan rectangular lesions on lower leaves
prevention: Resistant hybrids, crop rotation, residue management.

symptoms: circular brown spots with yellow halo on leaves
yellow streaks on leaves turning brown, leaf shredding
treatment: No cure. Remove and destroy infected plants. Treat soil with fungicide.
prevention: Disease-free suckers, crop rotation, resistant varieties.

symptoms: black spots on leaves and fruit, powdery growth
treatment: Apply copper fungicide or mancozeb at flowering and fruit set.
prevention: Prune for airflow, remove fallen fruit.

symptoms: yellowing, leaf drop, bronze colored mites visible
treatment: Apply abamectin or spiromesifen miticide. Spray neem oil weekly.

reason: Sandy loam has good drainage, suitable for root crops and oilseeds during warm season.
Black cotton soil retains moisture well, ideal for winter cereals and pulses.
Red laterite soil with high rainfall supports paddy and plantation crops.
Alluvial soil is highly fertile, supports a wide range of food crops.
Clay soil retains water, best for paddy and water-loving crops.
Sandy soil drains fast, drought-tolerant crops perform best here.
Loamy soil with good water retention is ideal for winter crops.
Salt-tolerant crops perform better in saline soils. Improve drainage first.

micronutrients: Zinc sulphate 25 kg/ha if deficiency observed.
tips: Split urea: 50% at transplanting, 25% at tillering, 25% at panicle initiation.

micronutrients: Boron 0.5 kg/ha for better grain development.
tips: Apply full P and K as basal. Split N in 3 doses.

organic: Mix 20-25 tonnes compost per hectare before planting.
micronutrients: Calcium and boron foliar spray to prevent blossom end rot.
tips: Use drip fertigation. Avoid excess nitrogen.

micronutrients: Zinc sulphate prevents white bud disease.
tips: Top dress 30 kg urea/ha at knee-high stage.

organic: Green manuring or FYM 10 tonnes/ha before sowing.
micronutrients: Boron 1 kg/ha foliar spray at boll formation.
tips: Avoid excess nitrogen — causes vegetative growth at expense of bolls.

organic: Press mud or FYM 25 tonnes/ha before planting.
micronutrients: Iron and zinc sulphate if chlorosis observed.
tips: Split nitrogen into 4-5 applications through the season.

organic: FYM 5 tonnes/ha. Gypsum 500 kg/ha at pegging.
micronutrients: Boron and sulphur for pod development.
tips: Gypsum is essential for pod filling. Treat seeds with Rhizobium.

micronutrients: Boron and calcium foliar spray for tuber quality.
tips: Split K: 50% basal, 50% at earthing up.

micronutrients: Sulphur 40 kg/ha for pungency and bulb size.
tips: Apply N in 3 splits. Stop irrigation 10-15 days before harvest.

organic: FYM or compost 10 kg per plant at planting.
micronutrients: Magnesium sulphate 100g/plant if leaf chlorosis observed.
tips: Apply K in multiple splits. High K demand at bunch development.

benefit: Rs 6000 per year in three equal instalments of Rs 2000 directly to farmer bank account.
eligibility: Small and marginal farmers with less than 2 hectares of land.
how_to_apply: Register at pmkisan.gov.in or visit nearest CSC.

benefit: Crop insurance covering losses due to natural calamities, pests, diseases. Premium only 1.5-2% for farmers.
eligibility: All farmers growing notified crops. Loanee farmers enrolled automatically.
how_to_apply: Apply through nearest bank branch, CSC, or pmfby.gov.in before cut-off date.

benefit: Short-term credit up to Rs 3 lakh at 4% interest rate for crop production and allied activities.
eligibility: All farmers, sharecroppers, oral lessees, and self-help groups.
how_to_apply: Apply at any nationalized bank, cooperative bank, or regional rural bank.

benefit: Free soil testing and personalized fertilizer recommendation report issued every 2 years.
how_to_apply: Contact local KVK or agriculture department office.

benefit: Subsidized drip and sprinkler irrigation systems. Up to 90% subsidy for small farmers.
eligibility: All farmers. Priority to water-scarce regions and small/marginal farmers.
how_to_apply: Apply through state agriculture department or pmksy.gov.in.

benefit: Online trading platform for farmers to sell produce directly across India at better prices.
how_to_apply: Register at enam.gov.in or at nearest APMC/mandi.

benefit: Rs 50,000 per hectare over 3 years for organic farming adoption. Includes training and certification.
eligibility: Farmers willing to adopt organic farming in clusters of 50 acres.
how_to_apply: Contact nearest KVK or state agriculture department.

benefit: Pension of Rs 3000 per month after age 60 for small and marginal farmers.
eligibility: Farmers aged 18-40 years with less than 2 hectares land.
how_to_apply: Register at CSC or maandhan.in with Aadhaar and bank details.

benefit: Free seeds, training, and input subsidies for rice, wheat, pulses, coarse cereals, and oilseeds.
how_to_apply: Contact block-level agriculture officer or district agriculture office.

advisory: Avoid spraying pesticides or fertilizers. Ensure proper drainage in fields. Harvest mature crops before rain if possible.
Use drip or sprinkler irrigation. Mulch the soil to reduce evaporation. Choose drought-tolerant varieties. Apply potassium fertilizer.
Irrigate crops in the evening to reduce heat stress. Apply mulching. Avoid fertilizing during peak heat.
Cover crops with polythene at night. Irrigate before frost to release heat. Avoid nitrogen fertilizer during cold spell.
Increase spacing for better airflow. Apply fungicides preventively. Avoid overhead irrigation. Scout for fungal diseases daily.
